content.rendered:
I seem to be in a groove lately with little-known artists covering Springsteen deep cuts, so let’s keep it going:

Today’s entry comes from Canadian folk singer Scott Jacobs, whose lovely acoustic guitar-and-pedal-steel arrangement of “Dry Lightning” beautifully captures Bruce’s tale of a disintegrating love affair.

“Dry Lightning” is a tough song to cover–Bruce’s deceptively gentle melody serves as counterpoint to the increasingly jaded lyrics. Deliver a vocal performance that’s too subtle, and the song sails by without ever registering its bite. Go too far, and the song loses the tenderness that gives the song its resonance.

Jacobs finds the right balance.

(But as a fellow Pacific Northwesterner, I can’t help but smile at Jacob’s choice of backdrop for his video: the seawall of his native Vancouver, B.C. on a gloriously and typically gray day. It’s tough to imagine dry lightning anywhere remotely around these parts.)

© November 27, 2019

excerpt.rendered: Canadian folk singer Marc Jacobs finds the soul of Bruce’s “Dry Lightning” and turns in a beautiful cover on a gray Vancouver day.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Jacob and Drinkwater: Dry Lightning
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Alt-folk duo Jacob and Drinkwater honored Bruce's sad, lovely deep cut with their acoustic cover performance on The Big Comfy Sessions.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Willie Nelson and Emmylou Harris: Dry Lightning
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Willie Nelson and Emmylou Harris hit a home run with their world-weary take on "Dry Lightning."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Dry Lightning
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The closes thing to an E Street Band track on The Ghost of Tom Joad, this post-romantic ballad points the way to Western Stars in more ways than one.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: Chain Lightning
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: The love child of "State Trooper" and "Pink Cadillac" (and grandchild of "Peter Gunn") "Chain Lightning" is a fascinating River cast-off that deserves a live outing.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Omaha
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Set the wayback machine to September 16, 1967, and we'll listen to 17-year-old Bruce Springsteen tear up Mobey Grape's "Omaha" in the earliest known live Springsteen recording.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Chimes of Freedom
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: When Bruce announced his participation in the Amnesty International Human Rights Now! Tour in 1988, his re-arrangement and performance of this Bob Dylan classic became the title track of an EP released the following month.
